title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Gwendolyn, Jiles, and Miss Marple discuss the mystery of a married man who may be a third "X" in a complicated history involving Helen and Walter Fane. Miss Marple suggests that Edith Pettigrew, a former cook at St. Catherine's Manor who lives in Dilham, might provide information. Gwendolyn decides to make a new will to get close to Walter Fane, despite Jiles's warnings.

In another location, Lily Kimball discovers an old newspaper advertisement asking anyone who knows Helen Spenceley-Harriet (née Kennedy) to contact Reed and Hardy in Southampton Street. Lily recalls serving Mrs. Harriet at St. Catherine's Manor, which was rented from Miss Finsbury and her husband. Lily speculates to her husband, Jim, that Mrs. Harriet may have been murdered by her husband, noting that a suitcase, handbag, and clothes went missing. She recalls that a Swiss nanny named Léonie saw something out the window during a chaotic night involving a doctor and a man sleeping in a dressing room. Lily believes Mrs. Harriet might have run off with a married lover, a theory she once shared with Edith, though Edith did not believe her.

Gwendolyn visits the law office of Walter Fane, a dignified, weary man of fifty. The office is an old-fashioned, Victorian-style space belonging to a senior partner. While discussing the signing of her simple will, Gwendolyn mentions that she and her companion have purchased a mountain villa that was formerly known as St. Catherine's Villa. Upon hearing this, Walter Fane removes his spectacles and looks down at the table.
